title: RPYR-0020 — `_asyncio._enter_task(loop, task)` Debug-formats an arbitrary `task` with `{:?}`, reaching the unsound `PyAtomicRef` Debug cast (RUSTPY-0018), so passing a function SIGSEGVs the interpreter (_asyncio.rs:2491 → object/ext.rs:278)
short_title: `_asyncio._enter_task` `{:?}` reaches the RUSTPY-0018 unsound Debug → SIGSEGV
one_line_repro:
import _asyncio, asyncio; l=asyncio.new_event_loop()
async def c(): pass
def g(): pass
_asyncio._enter_task(l, c())
_asyncio._enter_task(l, g)   # SIGSEGV
prior_art: A Python-reachable TRIGGER for fuzzer RUSTPY-0018 (the unsound PyAtomicRef Debug at object/ext.rs — a cross-method pointer-cast inconsistency: Debug casts the stored pointer to bare T while Deref/load_raw/From treat it as Py<T>). RUSTPY-0018 is the root; this is a new live pure-Python path into it. No specific RustPython issue.
found_by: debug-format-auditor (informed explore — the one live {:?}-on-pyobject trigger reaching the unsound Debug; the catalogued typevar/os triggers were traced to cosmetic)
unsafe-soundness-auditor (confirmed RUSTPY-0018 root still present and the only cross-method cast tree-wide)
toolkit_class: unsound-debug-trigger (memory-unsafety; native pointer misread → SIGSEGV, Class I gated on RUSTPY-0018)
cpython_behavior: CPython raises a clean RuntimeError (loop-state / already-running); it never dereferences a mis-cast pointer. RustPython SIGSEGVs.
guarded_twin: The root fix is RUSTPY-0018: `object/ext.rs:278` Debug does `.load().cast::<T>()` while Deref(307)/load_raw(320)/From all use `.cast::<Py<T>>()` — the stored pointer is a `Py<T>*`. One-char fix (`.cast::<Py<T>>()`) collapses the whole class to cosmetic. This trigger also warrants not Debug-formatting an arbitrary Python object in an error message (use repr).
affected: _asyncio._enter_task(loop, task) where task is a PyFunction (code: PyAtomicRef always populated) or any object whose Debug formats a populated PyAtomicRef
the RUSTPY-0018 unsound Debug reached via any {:?} on a PyFunction/Coro payload
notes: `_asyncio._enter_task` (a #[pyfunction]) formats the task argument with `{:?}` unconditionally in the 'a task is already running' error (_asyncio.rs:2491). The only payloads whose Debug actually formats a PyAtomicRef are PyFunction (`code: PyAtomicRef<PyCode>`, always populated) and Coro (`exception: PyAtomicRef`, None until set). So the crash needs a POPULATED PyAtomicRef -> pass a FUNCTION (a fresh coroutine's exception is None and prints harmlessly, taking the safe path). Verified live: enter a first task, then `_enter_task(loop, some_function)` -> the {:?} hits `object/ext.rs:278`'s `.cast::<T>()` on a `Py<PyCode>*`, misreads header words as payload -> garbage deref -> SIGSEGV (exit 139); CPython raises RuntimeError. FIX (root): RUSTPY-0018 one-char cast fix at ext.rs:278; also switch the error message from `{:?}` to Python repr. The catalogued typevar.rs/os.rs {:?} triggers are, on reachability, cosmetic (their reaching types always have __name__ -> safe path).
